opportunities.title: Beaver Works Summer Institute (BWSI)
opportunities.description: A rigorous 4-week STEM program at MIT for rising seniors. Students work on real engineering challenges in areas like autonomous vehicles, quantum computing, and cybersecurity. Highly competitive — strong math and science background required.

opportunities.title: Stanford Pre-Collegiate Summer Institutes
opportunities.description: Stanford's residential summer program for intellectually curious high schoolers. Courses span math, science, humanities, and social sciences taught by Stanford faculty and doctoral students. Financial aid is available.

opportunities.title: Yale Young Global Scholars (YYGS)
opportunities.description: An intensive two-week academic enrichment program at Yale for outstanding high school students worldwide. Tracks include Applied Science & Engineering, Biological & Biomedical Science, Politics Law & Economics, and more. Need-based financial aid available.

opportunities.title: Johns Hopkins Center for Talented Youth (CTY)
opportunities.description: One of the most established gifted youth programs in the country. Offers intensive 3-week courses in math, science, writing, and humanities on college campuses. Qualification through above-grade standardized testing required. Financial aid available.

opportunities.title: Carnegie Mellon Pre-College Programs
opportunities.description: CMU offers intensive pre-college programs in computer science, design, engineering, and the arts. Students experience college-level academics and campus life. Programs in AI, game design, architecture, and drama available.

opportunities.title: Brown Pre-College Programs
opportunities.description: Brown's pre-college offerings include courses in entrepreneurship, medicine, law, engineering, and the arts. Both residential and commuter options available. A great fit for self-directed learners who want college-style independence.

opportunities.title: CU Boulder Pre-Collegiate Development Program
opportunities.description: CU Boulder's summer programs for Colorado youth offer hands-on learning in engineering, aerospace, media arts, and entrepreneurship. Day and residential options available. Strong value for in-state students.

opportunities.title: Minority Engineering Summer Program (MESP)
opportunities.description: A free two-week residential engineering program at Colorado School of Mines for underrepresented high schoolers. Students live on campus, attend engineering classes, and tour industry facilities. Covers housing and meals.

opportunities.title: Teen Science Café Internship
opportunities.description: Teens work alongside DMNS staff to organize science café events, engage the public, and develop science communication skills. A stipend is provided. This is a year-round commitment with flexible hours around school.

opportunities.title: NASA HUNCH Program
opportunities.description: NASA's High school students United with NASA to Create Hardware (HUNCH) program lets students design and build real hardware used on the International Space Station. Teams compete nationally. School must register as a HUNCH site.

opportunities.title: Google CS First
opportunities.description: Google's free coding curriculum for beginners uses Scratch to teach computer science fundamentals. Clubs can run independently or be facilitated by a teacher or parent. Completely free with no prerequisites.

opportunities.title: iD Tech Camps
opportunities.description: Week-long tech camps covering coding, game design, AI, Roblox, and robotics. Held on college campuses across Colorado. Sibling discounts and financial aid available. Both day and overnight options.

opportunities.title: Youth Volunteer Program
opportunities.description: Douglas County Libraries offers year-round volunteer opportunities for teens including shelving books, assisting with programs, and supporting community events. Volunteer hours count toward school service requirements. Multiple branch locations available.

opportunities.title: Community Events Volunteer
opportunities.description: HRCA hosts over 200 community events per year and relies on youth volunteers to assist with setup, registration, activities, and cleanup. Great way to build leadership and community connections in Highlands Ranch.

opportunities.title: Neighbor Volunteer
opportunities.description: A Little Help connects volunteers of all ages with older adults who need support staying independent at home. Volunteers help with rides, yard work, errands, snow shoveling, tech help, and friendly check-in calls. Flexible commitment — a few hours a week or month works.

opportunities.title: Build Day Volunteer
opportunities.organization: Habitat for Humanity South Metro Denver
opportunities.description: Volunteers help build affordable homes in the South Metro Denver area alongside future homeowners. Youth 16+ can participate in build days on weekends. No construction experience needed — training provided on site.

opportunities.title: Child Development Volunteer
opportunities.description: Anchor Center serves young children who are blind or visually impaired. Volunteers assist in classrooms, support family programming, and help at fundraising events. Orientation required before first shift.

opportunities.title: Junior Volunteer Program
opportunities.description: Children's Hospital Colorado's junior volunteer program places teens in non-clinical support roles including child life, family support, and wayfinding. Requires a 100-hour commitment over summer. Competitive — apply early.

opportunities.title: Youth Mentor Volunteer
opportunities.description: SOS Outreach uses outdoor adventure to mentor underserved youth. Volunteers assist program staff on ski and outdoor trips, helping younger participants build confidence and resilience. Mountain experience helpful but not required.

opportunities.title: Animal Care Volunteer
opportunities.description: Colorado's largest animal shelter welcomes youth volunteers for animal socialization, kennel enrichment, and adoption event support. Orientation required. Shifts available on weekdays and weekends.

opportunities.title: Food Sorting Volunteer
opportunities.description: Volunteers sort and pack food donations at the warehouse. Great for school groups and individuals. Shifts are 3 hours and available on weekdays and weekends. Minimum age 13 with parental consent.

opportunities.title: Courtesy Clerk / Bagger
opportunities.description: King Soopers regularly hires teens 16+ as courtesy clerks. Responsibilities include bagging groceries, collecting carts, and assisting customers. Flexible scheduling around school. Multiple Highlands Ranch locations.

opportunities.title: Team Member
opportunities.description: Chick-fil-A is known as one of the best first employers for teens — structured training, closed Sundays, and scholarship opportunities for long-term employees. Front-of-house and kitchen roles available.

opportunities.title: Recreation Center Attendant
opportunities.description: HRCA's four recreation centers hire youth for front desk, fitness floor, and pool monitoring roles. Free rec center membership included as a benefit. Multiple positions open year-round.

opportunities.title: Golf Course Cart Attendant
opportunities.description: Seasonal position managing golf carts, maintaining the pro shop, and assisting golfers. Good fit for students who enjoy being outdoors. Tips supplement hourly wage. Season runs April through October.

opportunities.title: Paraprofessional / Classroom Aide
opportunities.description: DCSD hires paraprofessionals to support teachers in K-12 classrooms. Great for students pursuing education, psychology, or social work. Must be 18+ with a high school diploma. Benefits included for qualifying hours.

opportunities.title: Library Page
opportunities.description: Library pages shelve returned materials, assist patrons, and maintain organization across the library. Ideal for students who enjoy a quiet, structured environment. Evening and weekend shifts available.

opportunities.title: Guest Advocate / Cashier
opportunities.description: Target hires team members for checkout, sales floor, and fulfillment roles. Competitive starting wage, employee discount, and flexible scheduling. Multiple local locations with frequent openings.

opportunities.title: County Government Summer Internship
opportunities.description: Douglas County places interns in departments including planning, communications, public health, and administration. Paid summer positions for high school and college students. Strong application required — resume and letter of intent.

opportunities.title: High School Engineering Intern
opportunities.description: Lockheed Martin's Space division in Littleton occasionally offers summer internships for exceptional high school seniors. Positions focus on aerospace engineering, software, and systems design. Highly competitive — strong GPA and STEM coursework required.

opportunities.title: Healthcare Job Shadow Program
opportunities.description: Sky Ridge Medical Center's job shadow program connects aspiring healthcare professionals with physicians, nurses, and allied health staff. Students observe clinical and administrative operations. Background check required.

opportunities.title: High School Internship Program (HSIP)
opportunities.description: NREL's HSIP places high school students in paid summer research positions alongside scientists and engineers working on solar, wind, and energy storage technologies. Stipend provided. Strong math and science background required.

opportunities.title: Youth Commission Leadership Internship
opportunities.description: Intern with the Douglas County Youth Commission to support community programs, event coordination, and civic engagement initiatives. Build real leadership experience while connecting with county officials and community organizations.

opportunities.title: Summer Medical Pipeline Program
opportunities.description: CU Anschutz's pipeline program introduces underrepresented high schoolers to medical and health sciences careers. Students attend lectures, labs, and clinical simulations on a working medical campus. Free to attend.

opportunities.title: Colorado Uplift Youth Leadership Program
opportunities.description: Colorado Uplift runs free summer programs for Denver metro youth focused on leadership, character development, and college readiness. Small group mentorship model with adult coaches. Free meals and transportation provided.

opportunities.title: Game Day Staff
opportunities.description: Empower Field at Mile High hires seasonal game day staff for concessions, ushering, guest services, and merchandise. Shifts are on home game days (8–10 per season). Great experience and perks for sports fans.

opportunities.title: AWS Future Engineer Scholarship & Internship
opportunities.description: AWS Future Engineer provides scholarships and paid summer internships at Amazon for high school seniors from underrepresented communities interested in computer science. Includes a $40,000 college scholarship for recipients.

opportunities.title: Animal Welfare Advocate Volunteer
opportunities.description: Foothills Animal Shelter welcomes teen volunteers for dog walking, cat socialization, and adoption events. Youth 13–17 must have a parent volunteer alongside them for the first three months. Training provided.

opportunities.title: STEM Launch Peer Mentor
opportunities.description: STEM Launch K-8 school in Highlands Ranch recruits high school students to mentor younger students in STEM projects. Year-round commitment during the school year. Excellent college application experience in education and leadership.
